questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的毛利率是多少？经营性利润/亏损是多少？
questions.answer:
2024年，路特斯科技的毛利率为3.2%，相比2023年的15.0%有所下降。这一变化主要受到业务模式调整和外部经济环境的影响，尤其是在英国和欧洲市场从直营模式转向经销商模式，以及在不稳定的经济环境下进行的库存清理措施。尽管如此，路特斯科技在智能驾驶技术和高性能定制服务方面的持续创新，为其未来的盈利能力提供了坚实的基础。

在运营方面，2024年路特斯科技的运营亏损为7.86亿美元，较2023年的7.36亿美元增加了7%。尽管面临一定的财务压力，路特斯科技在2024年依然实现了总收入的显著增长，达到9.24亿美元，同比增长36%。这表明公司在市场扩展和产品交付方面取得了积极进展。

以上信息基于2025年4月30日之前的知识。

rag_info.sub_query: What is Lotus Technology's gross margin in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology's gross profits decreased from US$102.2 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, to US$29.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, and Lotus Technology's gross margin decreased from 15.0% for the year ended December 31, 2023, to 3.2% for the year ended December 31, 2024. The decrease in gross profit was primarily due to the reduction in gross profit from vehicle sales of US$78.3 million, resulting from a switch of Lotus Technology's business model from a direct-to-customer model to a dealer model in the UK and European markets and the destocking under an uncertain economic environment, partially offset by the decrease in charging and aftersales service of US$5.7 million. The decrease in gross margin was primarily due to the reduction in vehicle sales margins as more discounts and rebates were provided to dealers and customers under destocking measures.

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Exhibit No.   Description 
summary: Lotus Technology has revealed promising preliminary delivery results for 2024, showcasing a notable rise in vehicle deliveries driven by robust demand for its electric vehicles. This increase is linked to improved production capabilities and effective marketing strategies. The company is focused on maintaining this growth trajectory and enhancing its market presence in the near future. Additionally, they provided insights into specific models and future projections, underscoring their dedication to innovation and customer satisfaction.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for
the Full Year of 2024 
summary: In the most recent financial report for the full year ending December 31, 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $924 million, reflecting a substantial 36% increase from the previous year. This growth was primarily driven by a 35% rise in goods sales, which accounted for $891 million, alongside a remarkable 77% increase in service revenues, totaling $33 million. Despite this revenue growth, the company faced challenges with profitability, as the gross margin plummeted to 3%, down from 15% in 2023. The gross margin for goods sales decreased to 3% from 14%, while service revenues saw a decline to 17% from 36%.

The financial struggles were further highlighted by an operating loss of $786 million, marking a 7% increase year-over-year, and a net loss of $1,107 million, which represented a significant 48% rise compared to the prior year. Non-GAAP measures indicated an adjusted net loss of $1,075 million, a 43% increase, and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $961 million, reflecting a 39% increase in losses. Additionally, the cost of revenues surged by 55% to $895 million, resulting in a gross profit of only $29 million. The report also includes references to non-GAAP financial measures and emphasizes the importance of understanding the adjustments made to these figures, with further details provided in the accompanying notes.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the first half of 2024, technology-related revenue began to scale as Lotus Technology started to provide intelligent driving solutions and R&D services to multiple leading automotive brands. As the fleet of models equipped with state-of-the-art hardware continues to expand, paid software subscriptions represent a potential growth driver in the future. The Chapman Bespoke service, which provides customer personalization, design customizations, limited collection editions, and one-off models, was also launched to cater to growing demand. Deliveries of bespoke models are expected to further boost Lotus Technology’s luxury positioning and contribute to the company’s long-term premium gross margin. After assessment of the evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the US and EU, Lotus Technology has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

- **Row 6**: Net loss for first half 2024 was $460 million, which is 30% higher than the $353 million reported for first half 2023.  
- **Row 7**: Adjusted net loss (A) for first half 2024 was $424 million, a 20% increase from $353 million in first half 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the company achieved remarkable financial growth, reporting total revenues of $398 million, which marks a substantial 206% increase from $130 million in the same period of 2023. This growth was primarily driven by a significant rise in sales of goods, totaling $383 million (up 207%), while service revenues reached $15 million (up 194%). The gross profit also saw a dramatic increase to $51 million, reflecting a 731% rise, with the gross margin improving to 13%, compared to 5% in the prior year.

Despite these positive revenue and profit trends, the company faced significant challenges, reporting an operating loss of $438 million, a 27% increase from the previous year's loss of $344 million. The net loss for the period was $460 million, which is 30% higher than the $353 million loss reported in the first half of 2023. Additionally, the adjusted net loss was $424 million, up 20% year-over-year, while adjusted EBITDA reflected a loss of $382 million, a 15% increase from the previous year's loss of $333 million.

Overall, while the company demonstrated strong revenue and gross profit growth, it also contended with escalating losses and expenses, indicative of its ongoing expansion efforts and strategic investments.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Gross profit and gross margin 
summary: The financial analysis for the years 2022 to 2024 reveals a significant decline in gross profit and gross margin, particularly in 2024. Gross profit dropped to $29.6 million from $102.2 million in 2023, marking a staggering decrease of 71%. Correspondingly, the gross margin fell from 15.0% in 2023 to just 3.2% in 2024. This downturn is primarily attributed to a substantial decline in vehicle sales, which fell by $78.3 million due to a strategic shift from direct sales to a dealer model in the UK and European markets, alongside inventory destocking amid economic uncertainty. The reduction in gross margin was exacerbated by increased discounts and rebates offered to dealers and customers.

Operating expenses also underwent notable changes during this period. Research and development expenses decreased by $93.9 million, reflecting a reduction in activities related to specific vehicle models. Selling and marketing expenses saw a slight decline, driven by cost efficiencies and the transition to a dealer model. Conversely, general and administrative expenses surged by $82.9 million, largely due to increased payroll and professional service costs associated with expanding operations in Europe. 

Despite these challenges, government grants received nearly doubled, providing some financial relief. Overall, total operating expenses for 2024 were reduced slightly compared to 2023, but the company still reported an operational loss of $786.3 million, an increase from the previous year's loss of $735.9 million. This comprehensive overview highlights the critical shifts in financial performance and operational strategies during this period.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's gross profits decreased from US$102.2 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, to US$29.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, and Lotus Technology's gross margin decreased from 15.0% for the year ended December 31, 2023, to 3.2% for the year ended December 31, 2024. The decrease in gross profit was primarily due to the reduction in gross profit from vehicle sales of US$78.3 million, resulting from a switch of Lotus Technology's business model from a direct-to-customer model to a dealer model in the UK and European markets and the destocking under an uncertain economic environment, partially offset by the decrease in charging and aftersales service of US$5.7 million. The decrease in gross margin was primarily due to the reduction in vehicle sales margins as more discounts and rebates were provided to dealers and customers under destocking measures.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Over 12,000 vehicles delivered in 2024, a year-on-year increase of over 70%. Total revenue reached $924 million, a 36% year-on-year growth. Achieved a reduction in operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through rigorous execution of efficiency enhancement initiatives. Lotus Technology is expected to consolidate the entire global business of the Lotus brand by acquisition of a majority interest in Lotus UK upon the closing of Gee. New York – April 22, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter
and Full Year 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, achieved significant milestones by delivering over 12,000 vehicles, which represents a remarkable year-on-year increase of more than 70%. The company's total revenue reached $924 million, reflecting a 36% growth from the previous year. Furthermore, it successfully reduced operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through various efficiency enhancement initiatives. Looking to the future, Lotus plans to strengthen its global presence by acquiring a majority interest in Lotus UK following the completion of the Gee transaction. The unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the full year ending December 31, 2024, were announced on April 22, 2025, in New York.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited
Second Quarter and First Half 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the brand experienced its best performance to date, driven by a well-balanced global strategy and significant deliveries in the US after its market re-entry. The introduction of tailored services and growth in technology-related revenue are expected to improve profit margins in the future. However, the annual delivery target has been revised due to short-term challenges from trade uncertainties and tariff hikes. Additionally, the brand has launched the "Win26" Plan, which aims for positive operating cash flow and EBITDA by 2026. Lotus Technology Inc. also reported its unaudited financial results for the second quarter and the first half of 2024, highlighting its position in the global intelligent and luxury mobility sector, although specific financial details were not disclosed.

Lotus Technology also achieved a balanced global allocation with each region contributing 20-30% of the total deliveries in the first half of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The US market recorded exceptional growth and contributed 26% of the total deliveries after Lotus Technology's re-entry into the market. The achievements reflect Lotus Technology’s steady transition from a traditional sportscar maker to an intelligent luxury mobility provider. Deliveries by Model Type

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – January 14, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology Inc. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Preliminary Delivery
Results of 2024 
summary: On January 14, 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving an impressive total of 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting a year-on-year growth of over 70%, the highest among luxury brands. Notably, around 25% of these deliveries were made in China. Looking ahead to 2025, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20%, with China expected to contribute 25-28% to this growth. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including its iconic LOTUS branding, ensuring consistent global representation. Additionally, the delivery figures reported for the US market are based on company business unit reports and may differ slightly from financial accounting records. Lotus is positioned alongside other prestigious luxury brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, and Rolls Royce, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and performance in the automotive industry.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive loss 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its financial performance for the years ending December 31, 2024, and 2023, highlighting significant changes in revenue, costs, and losses. In 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $924.3 million, a notable increase from $679 million in 2023, primarily due to higher sales of goods and services. However, the cost of revenues also surged to $894.7 million from $576.8 million, resulting in a gross profit decline to $29.6 million from $102.2 million the previous year.

Operating expenses decreased slightly to $815.9 million in 2024 from $838.1 million in 2023, with reductions in research and development and selling and marketing costs. Despite these efforts, the operating loss grew to $786.3 million, up from $735.9 million in 2023. The company also faced increased interest expenses, leading to a net loss before income taxes of $1.1 billion in 2024, compared to $749.1 million in the prior year. The overall net loss for 2024 reached $1.1 billion, an increase from $750.3 million in 2023, with the loss per ordinary share rising to ($1.72) from ($1.60).

These results indicate that while Lotus Technology experienced substantial revenue growth, it also encountered rising costs and deepening losses, reflecting ongoing challenges. All figures are in thousands of US dollars, and adjustments for recapitalization following a merger in February 2024 have been made.

- Table Title: Operational Benchmarking for Comparable Luxury Car Companies
- Table Summary: The table compares key financial metrics of Lotus Technology with several luxury and high-performance car companies, focusing on estimated revenue growth rate (CAGR) from 2023 to 2025, as well as projected gross and EBITDA margins for 2024 and 2025. These estimates are based on analyst forecasts and publicly available data as of January 30, 2023.
- Context: The table is part of a larger analysis where the LCAA Board evaluated financial forecasts and metrics of Lotus Technology alongside other luxury automotive brands, considering factors such as brand heritage, product performance, and market pricing.
- Special Notes: All figures are estimates (noted as E) and projections. Gross Margin percentages for Lucid in 2024E and EBITDA margin in 2025E have negative values, indicating costs exceeding revenue.

[Row Level]
Row 1: Lotus Technology is expected to achieve a revenue CAGR of approximately 90% from 2023 to 2025. For 2024, the gross margin is projected to range between 18.0% and 20.0%, and for 2025, it is expected to be between 21.0% and 23.0%. The EBITDA margin in 2025 is anticipated to be greater than 5.0%.

Row 2: Tesla's estimated revenue CAGR from 2023 to 2025 is 28.4%. In 2024, Tesla's gross margin is projected at 21.5%, improving to 23.2% by 2025, with an EBITDA margin of 21% in 2025.

Row 3: Lucid anticipates a revenue CAGR of 125.7% from 2023 to 2025. However, Lucid's gross margin for 2024 is forecasted at a negative 15.4%, with a slight improvement to 8.1% in 2025, but with a negative EBITDA margin of 10.2% in 2025.

Row 4: Polestar's revenue is expected to grow at a CAGR of 79.8% from 2023 to 2025. Polestar's gross margin is estimated at 8.3% for 2024, rising to 14.7% in 2025, with a minimal EBITDA margin of 0.8% in 2025.

Row 5: NIO is projected to have a revenue CAGR of 39.3% during the 2023 to 2025 period. NIO's gross margin is expected to be 15% in 2024 and 17.2% in 2025, along with an EBITDA margin of 1.6% in 2025.

Row 6: Ferrari's revenue growth is estimated at a CAGR of 8.5% from 2023 to 2025. Ferrari's gross margin in 2024 is projected to be 51.2%, with a slight increase to 51.9% in 2025, and the EBITDA margin is forecasted at 39.2% in 2025.

Row 7: Porsche's anticipated revenue CAGR is 6.5% over the 2023 to 2025 period. For 2024, Porsche's gross margin is projected at 28.3%, with a slight decline to 28.1% in 2025, and an EBITDA margin of 25.7% in 2025.

Row 8: Aston Martin expects a revenue CAGR of 12.4% from 2023 to 2025. Aston Martin projects a gross margin of 36.2% in 2024, rising to 38.7% in 2025, along with an EBITDA margin of 24.5% in 2025.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational Benchmarking 
summary: The LCAA Board conducted a comprehensive analysis of Lotus Tech's financial metrics, including estimated revenue growth, gross margins, and EBITDA margins, in relation to comparable luxury and high-performance automotive brands. This evaluation utilized Wall Street analyst consensus forecasts for the years 2023 to 2025, based on publicly available data as of January 30, 2023.

Lotus Tech is projected to achieve an impressive revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 90% from 2023 to 2025, with gross margins expected to range from 18.0% to 20.0% in 2024 and 21.0% to 23.0% in 2025. The EBITDA margin is anticipated to exceed 5.0% by 2025. In comparison, other brands show varied performance: Tesla is expected to have a CAGR of 28.4% and strong margins; Lucid forecasts a high CAGR of 125.7% but struggles with negative margins; Polestar and NIO project moderate growth with low margins; while established brands like Ferrari and Porsche maintain robust margins but lower growth rates.

Additionally, the analysis included an evaluation of Lotus Tech's estimated enterprise value-to-sales (EV/Sales) multiples, which are projected to be significantly lower than those of its competitors. For 2024 and 2025, Lotus Tech's multiples are estimated at approximately 0.9x and 0.6x, respectively. In contrast, Tesla leads with multiples of 7.2x and 5.6x, while Ferrari exhibits the highest multiples at 8.4x and 7.7x. Overall, Lotus Tech's lower projected multiples suggest a potential undervaluation compared to its established peers in the automotive sector, despite its promising growth trajectory.

- **Table Title**: Comparable Company Analysis - Enterprise Value to Sales Multiples (2024E and 2025E)  
- **Table Summary**: This table compares the estimated Enterprise Value (EV)-to-Sales multiples of Lotus Technology, based on its Initial Projections, to those of comparable companies in the automotive and luxury automobile sectors. The multiples for 2024 and 2025 are presented, reflecting Wall Street analyst consensus forecasts as of January 30, 2023.  
- **Context**: The table is part of an analysis conducted by the LCAA Board to compare Lotus Technology's projected financial metrics to a peer group of companies, using publicly available data and consensus forecasts. Projections were based on available information as of January 30, 2023, and were integral to the evaluation of the Business Combination with Lotus Technology.  
- **Special Notes**: The symbol "~" is used for approximate values. All values are expressed as multiples (e.g., 0.9x).  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: Lotus Technology, based on its Initial Projections, has an estimated Enterprise Value-to-Sales multiple of approximately 0.9x for 2024 and approximately 0.6x for 2025.  
- **Row 2**: Tesla has significantly higher Enterprise Value-to-Sales multiples compared to Lotus Technology, with a 2024E multiple of 7.2x and a 2025E multiple of 5.6x.  
- **Row 3**: Lucid’s EV-to-Sales multiples are 5.0x for 2024E and decrease to 2.5x for 2025E, still higher than Lotus Technology’s projections.  
- **Row 4**: Polestar is projected to have a 2024E multiple of 1.7x and a 2025E multiple of 0.9x, which are closer to Lotus Technology’s lower-end estimates but still higher.  
- **Row 5**: NIO’s estimated multiples are lower than Tesla’s but remain above Lotus Technology’s, with a 2024E multiple of 1.3x and a 2025E multiple of 1.0x.  
- **Row 6**: Ferrari shows the highest EV-to-Sales multiples among the group, with an 8.4x multiple for 2024E and 7.7x for 2025E, vastly exceeding Lotus Technology’s projections.  
- **Row 7**: Porsche has comparable 2024 and 2025 multiples at 2.5x and 2.4x, respectively, higher than Lotus Technology but notably lower than luxury-oriented peers like Ferrari.  
- **Row 8**: Aston Martin’s EV-to-Sales multiples are 1.9x for 2024E and decrease slightly to 1.7x for 2025E, exceeding Lotus Technology’s values but closer in range compared to other automakers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
Robust start in 2024, marking the strongest first half-year performance in Lotus Technology's history.  
Balanced global allocation, with strong US deliveries contributing after the brand’s re-entry into the market.  
Launched bespoke services and continued growing technology-related income, which are expected to be potential contributors to profit margin in the future.  
Adjusted the annual delivery target due to near-term disruptions from trade uncertainties and tariff hikes.  
Initiated the “Win26” Plan aiming to achieve positive operating cash flow and EBITDA in 2026. New York – August 28, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the second quarter and half year ended June 30, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited
Second Quarter and First Half 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported its strongest performance to date, bolstered by a well-balanced global allocation and significant deliveries in the US following its market re-entry. The company anticipates that the introduction of bespoke services and growth in technology-related income will further enhance profit margins. However, it has adjusted its annual delivery target due to short-term challenges related to trade uncertainties and tariff increases. Additionally, the brand has initiated the "Win26" Plan, which aims to achieve positive operating cash flow and EBITDA by 2026. The unaudited financial results for the second quarter and the first half of 2024, ending June 30, were disclosed in a report released on August 28, 2024, highlighting Lotus's position as a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED STATEMENTS OF COMPREHENSIVE LOSS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024, 2023 AND 2022 (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: The financial performance of Lotus Technology Inc. over the years ending December 31 for 2024, 2023, and 2022 reveals significant trends and challenges. In 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $921,049, a notable increase from $660,198 in 2023 and $95,577 in 2022, primarily driven by sales of goods and services. However, the cost of revenues surged to $891,123, resulting in a gross profit of only $29,926, compared to $102,181 in 2023 and $2,307 in 2022, indicating a sharp rise in costs relative to revenue growth.

Operating expenses for 2024 totaled $815,949, with substantial investments in research and development, selling and marketing, and general and administrative functions, leading to an operating loss of $786,322. The company reported a net loss of $1,107,389 for the year, with a loss attributable to ordinary shareholders of $1,104,925, translating to a loss per share of $1.72 based on a weighted average of 645,227,356 shares outstanding.

After factoring in other comprehensive income adjustments of $29,898, the total comprehensive loss for 2024 amounted to $1,077,391. This summary highlights the ongoing financial challenges faced by Lotus Technology Inc. and provides critical insights for investors and stakeholders regarding the company's financial trajectory. All figures are presented in thousands of US dollars, with share data reflecting adjustments for Recapitalization.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

- **Table Summary:** This table presents the financial performance of Lotus Technology Inc. for the first half (first half) and second quarter (2Q) of fiscal year 2024 (FY2024) compared to corresponding periods in FY2023. Metrics include deliveries, revenues, cost of revenues, gross profit, gross profit margin, operating loss, net loss, adjusted net loss, and adjusted EBITDA, with year-over-year (YoY) percentage changes provided.  
- **Context:** The table details unaudited financial results to analyze performance trends over time, emphasizing significant year-over-year growth and challenges across various metrics.  
- **Special Notes:** All values are in millions of US dollars, unless otherwise noted. Unit measurements like deliveries are in units, and percentages for margins and YoY changes are explicitly listed. Footnotes 1 and 2 reference specific calculations or adjustments.  

---

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1:** Deliveries in 1H2024 reached 4,873 units, compared to 1,439 units in 1H2023, reflecting an impressive 239% YoY increase. In 2Q2024, deliveries totaled 2,693 units, up 128% from 1,175 units in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 2:** Revenues grew significantly to $398 million in 1H2024 from $130 million in 1H2023, representing a 206% YoY increase. Revenues in 2Q2024 amounted to $225 million, a 103% increase from $111 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 3:** The cost of revenues increased to $347 million in 1H2024 from $124 million in 1H2023, a 180% YoY rise. In 2Q2024, the cost of revenues was $204 million, showing a 95% increase compared to $105 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 4:** Gross profit in 1H2024 improved to $51 million, up substantially from $6 million in 1H2023, marking a 731% YoY growth. In 2Q2024, gross profit was $21 million, a 246% increase from $6 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 5:** The gross profit margin for 1H2024 stood at 13%, significantly higher than the 5% recorded in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the margin reached 9%, compared to 5% in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 6:** Operating loss expanded to $438 million in 1H2024, a 27% increase from $344 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the operating loss was $204 million, compared to $183 million in 2Q2023, marking a 12% YoY growth.  
- **Row 7:** Net loss for 1H2024 reached $460 million, a 30% increase from $353 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, net loss stood at $202 million, 4% higher than $193 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 8:** Adjusted net loss in 1H2024 was $424 million, rising 20% from $353 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, adjusted net loss was $201 million, compared to $193 million in 2Q2023, a 4% increase.  
- **Row 9:** Adjusted EBITDA in 1H2024 stood at a loss of $382 million, up 15% from $333 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, it was a loss of $177 million, almost unchanged (a 1% decrease) from $178 million in 2Q2023.

summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

- **Row 4**: By 2026 (Win26), Lotus Technology Inc. targets delivering over 30,000 units while achieving positive operating cash flow (+VE OCF).  
- **Row 5**: By 2028 (Vision80), the strategic focus includes capturing 4% of the segment market share.

[Table Level]
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

[Row Level]

- **Row 1**: As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc.'s cash assets are $268,781, compared to $418,941 on December 31, 2023.  
- **Row 2**: Restricted cash amounts to $373,887 as of June 30, 2024, significantly higher than $7,873 as of December 31, 2023.

- **Row 10**: Investment securities (related parties) decreased to $2,168 on June 30, 2024, from $3,326 at the end of 2023.

- **Row 15**: Operating lease right-of-use assets reduced slightly to $158,864 on June 30, 2024, from $173,103 on December 31, 2023.  
- **Row 16**: Other non-current assets (third parties) increased to $65,792 on June 30, 2024, from $50,533 at the end of 2023.  
- **Row 17**: Other non-current assets (related parties) rose slightly to $2,689 as of June 30, 2024, from $2,706 at year-end 2023.  
- **Row 18**: Total non-current assets grew to $1,227,041 on June 30, 2024, from $700,966 on December 31, 2023.  
- **Row 19**: Total assets increased to $2,506,254 as of June 30, 2024, from $1,584,678 at the end of 2023.  

**Liabilities, Mezzanine Equity, and Shareholders' Deficit Section**  

- **Row 20**: Current liabilities include short-term borrowings from third parties, which surged to $518,479 as of June 30, 2024, compared to $226,777 at year-end 2023.

- **Row 26**: Accrued expenses and other current liabilities (related parties) decreased to $243,024 on June 30, 2024, from $290,668 at the end of 2023.  
- **Row 27**: Total current liabilities rose to $1,840,454 on June 30, 2024, compared to $1,757,276 at the end of 2023.  
- **Row 28**: Exchangeable notes remain unchanged at $378,638 in both periods.  
- **Row 29**: Convertible notes (related parties) totaled $110,661 as of June 30, 2024, with no record for December 31, 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 11. What is the gross margin outlook and reasons? How will the company improve profit margins in the future?
summary: The company is strategically positioned to achieve positive EBITDA and cash flow by 2026 through its Win26 program, which incorporates a series of targeted initiatives aimed at enhancing gross margins and overall profitability. Central to this strategy is the maintenance of a luxury brand positioning and a unified pricing strategy designed to protect brand value and elevate brand desirability. This approach also focuses on increasing the value of used cars, thereby enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

To cater to diverse global markets, the company plans to adjust its product offerings by introducing high-performance special editions and limited editions. These initiatives are expected to resonate with various consumer segments, driving sales and improving margins. Additionally, the company is committed to optimizing its operations and organizational structure, which will facilitate the implementation of cost-saving measures without compromising the premium customer experience that is synonymous with its brand.

Overall, the Win26 program represents a comprehensive approach to improving profit margins, leveraging brand strength, product innovation, and operational efficiency to secure a sustainable financial future. The company's focus on these key areas is expected to yield significant improvements in gross margin outlook as it moves toward its 2026 financial goals.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Non-GAAP Financial Measures 
summary: In the evaluation of our operating results, we focus on key non-GAAP financial measures, specifically adjusted net loss and adjusted EBITDA. These measures are crucial for management's assessment of operating performance and business planning, as they help identify underlying trends that may be obscured by certain expenses included in net loss. Adjusted net loss excludes share-based compensation expenses, which do not impact income tax, while adjusted EBITDA further excludes interest income, interest expenses, income tax expenses, depreciation, and share-based compensation.

For the six months ended June 30, 2024, the company reported a net loss of $(460,252) thousand, an increase from $(352,942) thousand in the same period of 2023. The adjusted net loss for this period was $(424,358) thousand, compared to $(352,942) thousand in 2023. Notably, share-based compensation expenses amounted to $35,894 thousand for the first half of 2024, with no expenses recorded in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the six months ended June 30, 2024, was $(381,667) thousand, compared to $(332,697) thousand in 2023.

Revenue for the same period totaled $398,115 thousand, representing a substantial increase of $268,080 thousand or 206.2% from $130,035 thousand in 2023. This growth was primarily driven by increased sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models and sports cars. Sales of goods revenue rose to $382,893 thousand, an increase of 206.7%, while service revenues increased to $15,222 thousand, reflecting a rise of 193.8%.

The cost of revenues for the six months ended June 30, 2024, was $347,203 thousand, marking a 180.2% increase from $123,908 thousand in 2023, largely due to higher vehicle sales. Consequently, gross profit for this period was $50,912 thousand, up by $44,785 thousand or 730.9%, resulting in a gross margin of 12.8%, compared to 4.7% in 2023. This improvement was attributed to cost reductions per vehicle, increased average selling prices, and higher-margin service income.

Overall, the financial metrics indicate significant growth in revenue and gross profit, while the adjusted measures provide valuable insights into underlying operational performance. This comprehensive analysis facilitates a clearer assessment for investors, highlighting the company's robust growth trajectory despite the increase in net loss and adjusted net loss.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

Despite these advancements, Lotus Technology faces challenges reflected in its financial statements, including a net loss of $258 million in Q1 2024, which marks a significant increase from the previous year. The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has not been profitable since its inception. Lotus Technology incurred a net loss of US\$1,107.3 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$724.6 million in 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$848.5 million, US\$386.9 million, and US\$351.4 million in 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. As of December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022, Lotus Technology's accumulated deficit was US\$2.7 billion, US\$1.6 billion, and US\$846.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$57.3 million, US\$213.7 million, and US\$133.3 million in 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of equity securities, exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. Lotus Technology will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations. These strategies may include, but are not limited to, obtaining additional loans from banks or related parties, renewing existing loans when they are due, and improving operational efficiency to grow revenues and control expenses. The feasibility of such plans is contingent upon many factors out of Lotus Technology's control and is highly uncertain and difficult to predict. Lotus Technology may also seek additional equity or debt financing in the future to satisfy capital requirements, respond to adverse developments or changes in its circumstances or unforeseen events or conditions, or fund organic or inorganic growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We have not been profitable and had negative net cash flows from operations. If we do not effectively manage our cash and other liquid financial assets, execute our plan to increase profitability and obtain additional financing, we may not be able to continue as a going concern. 
summary: The company has struggled with persistent financial losses since its establishment, reporting net losses of $1.1 billion, $750.3 million, and $724.6 million for the years 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. It has also faced negative cash flows from operating activities totaling $848.5 million, $386.9 million, and $351.4 million during the same period, leading to an accumulated deficit of $2.7 billion, $1.6 billion, and $846.8 million by the end of each year. Despite incurring capital expenditures of $57.3 million, $213.7 million, and $133.3 million over the three years, the company has relied heavily on various financing methods, including equity securities and related party borrowings, to sustain its operations.

To navigate the next 12 months, the company seeks additional liquidity and is considering funding strategies such as loans and enhancing operational efficiency. However, the effectiveness of these strategies is uncertain and contingent on external factors. The need for further equity or debt financing to address capital requirements or unforeseen challenges poses additional risks, as favorable financing terms are not guaranteed, potentially jeopardizing operational viability.

The company is under significant pressure to achieve positive cash flow while managing various contractual obligations, including derivative arrangements and debts. Although it plans to invest in increasing vehicle production, expanding its sales and service network, and advancing research and development, there are concerns that these investments may not yield immediate revenue growth or positive cash flow. This situation raises doubts about the company's ability to continue as a going concern, which could further restrict access to favorable financing options and increase the risk of substantial investment losses for shareholders. Additionally, the company faces significant risks that may impede its revenue generation, including insufficient demand for its vehicles, rising competition, and unexpected expenses, complicating its path to profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We have not been profitable and had negative net cash flows from operations. If we do not effectively manage our cash and other liquid financial assets, execute our plan to increase profitability and obtain additional financing, we may not be able to continue as a going concern. 
summary: The company has faced significant financial challenges since its inception, reporting substantial net losses totaling $460.3 million, $750.3 million, $724.6 million, and $110.5 million for the six months ended June 30 in 2024, 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Alongside these losses, the company has experienced negative net cash flows from operating activities of $468.4 million, $386.9 million, $351.4 million, and $126.5 million for the same periods. As of June 30, 2024, the accumulated deficit reached $2.0 billion, a significant increase from $1.6 billion in December 2023, $846.8 million in 2022, and $122.8 million in 2021. Capital expenditures have also been substantial, amounting to $49.5 million, $213.7 million, $133.3 million, and $34.6 million for the respective six-month periods.

To finance its operations, the company has primarily depended on proceeds from exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings. However, it requires additional liquidity over the next 12 months and is actively exploring various funding strategies, including loans and enhancing operational efficiency. The feasibility of these plans remains uncertain and is influenced by factors outside the company's control.

The company is under pressure to generate positive cash flow due to existing contractual obligations and anticipates ongoing investments in vehicle production, sales network expansion, and research and development (R&D). However, these investments may not result in timely revenue increases or positive cash flow. If the company fails to continue as a going concern, it may encounter difficulties in securing financing on acceptable terms, which raises substantial doubt about its operational viability. Furthermore, the financial statements do not account for potential adjustments should the company be unable to maintain normal operations, potentially leading to significant losses for investors. The company also faces risks such as lack of demand, increasing competition, and unforeseen expenses, all of which may impede its ability to generate revenue and achieve profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for
the Full Year of 2024 
summary: In the most recent financial report for the full year ending December 31, 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $924 million, reflecting a substantial 36% increase from the previous year. This growth was primarily driven by a 35% rise in goods sales, which accounted for $891 million, alongside a remarkable 77% increase in service revenues, totaling $33 million. Despite this revenue growth, the company faced challenges with profitability, as the gross margin plummeted to 3%, down from 15% in 2023. The gross margin for goods sales decreased to 3% from 14%, while service revenues saw a decline to 17% from 36%.

The financial struggles were further highlighted by an operating loss of $786 million, marking a 7% increase year-over-year, and a net loss of $1,107 million, which represented a significant 48% rise compared to the prior year. Non-GAAP measures indicated an adjusted net loss of $1,075 million, a 43% increase, and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $961 million, reflecting a 39% increase in losses. Additionally, the cost of revenues surged by 55% to $895 million, resulting in a gross profit of only $29 million. The report also includes references to non-GAAP financial measures and emphasizes the importance of understanding the adjustments made to these figures, with further details provided in the accompanying notes.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Over 12,000 vehicles delivered in 2024, a year-on-year increase of over 70%. Total revenue reached $924 million, a 36% year-on-year growth. Achieved a reduction in operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through rigorous execution of efficiency enhancement initiatives. Lotus Technology is expected to consolidate the entire global business of the Lotus brand by acquisition of a majority interest in Lotus UK upon the closing of Gee. New York – April 22, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter
and Full Year 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, achieved significant milestones by delivering over 12,000 vehicles, which represents a remarkable year-on-year increase of more than 70%. The company's total revenue reached $924 million, reflecting a 36% growth from the previous year. Furthermore, it successfully reduced operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through various efficiency enhancement initiatives. Looking to the future, Lotus plans to strengthen its global presence by acquiring a majority interest in Lotus UK following the completion of the Gee transaction. The unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the full year ending December 31, 2024, were announced on April 22, 2025, in New York.

title: Forward-Looking Statements 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Financial Performance for Lotus Technology Inc. (as of September 30, 2024)

This press release includes forward-looking statements as defined by the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, which are subject to risks and uncertainties outlined in the company's SEC filings. The information is current as of the release date, and Lotus Tech is not obligated to update these statements unless legally required.

#### Financial Overview

For the nine months ended September 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported significant financial changes compared to the same period in 2023:

- **Total Revenues:** Increased to $652,823,000 in 2024 from $317,941,000 in 2023.
  - **Sales Revenues:** Rose to $624,249,000 (2024) from $305,480,000 (2023).
  - **Service Revenues:** Increased to $28,574,000 (2024) from $12,461,000 (2023).
  
- **Total Costs of Revenues:** Increased to $593,708,000 in 2024 from $284,193,000 in 2023.
  - **Cost of Goods Sold:** Rose to $580,820,000 (2024) from $275,246,000 (2023).
  - **Cost of Services:** Increased to $12,888,000 (2024) from $8,947,000 (2023).
  
- **Gross Profit:** Increased to $59,115,000 in 2024 from $33,748,000 in 2023.

- **Total Operating Expenses:** Increased to $656,860,000 in 2024 from $541,624,000 in 2023.
  - **R&D Expenses:** Slightly decreased to $227,525,000 (2024) from $234,547,000 (2023).
  - **Selling and Marketing Expenses:** Increased to $259,804,000 (2024) from $191,231,000 (2023).
  - **General and Administrative Expenses:** Increased to $175,342,000 (2024) from $118,203,000 (2023).

- **Operating Loss:** Increased to $597,745,000 in 2024 from $507,876,000 in 2023.
- **Net Loss:** Increased to $666,520,000 in 2024 from $526,524,000 in 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: For the nine months ended September 30, 2024, Lotus reported a revenue of $652,823,000, a significant increase from $317,941,000 for the same period in 2023. The cost of revenues rose to $593,708,000 from $284,193,000, resulting in a gross profit of $59,115,000 compared to $33,748,000 in the previous year. Total operating expenses for the period amounted to $656,860,000, a rise from $541,624,000 in 2023. This included research and development expenses of $227,525,000 and selling and marketing expenses of $259,804,000. General and administrative expenses were $175,342,000. The company received government grants of $5,811,000, up from $2,357,000 in 2023. Operating loss increased to $597,745,000 from $507,876,000 the previous year. Interest expenses were $20,557,000, but the company gained $15,276,000 in interest income and $10,799,000 in investment income. Net losses before income taxes were $665,365,000, leading to a net loss attributable to ordinary shareholders of $665,118,000.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. Operating Results 
summary: We are a leading global provider of luxury mobility solutions, focusing on the design and development of sophisticated lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." Our product lineup includes a comprehensive range of Lotus-branded cars, reflecting our commitment to quality in the luxury automotive sector. In 2024, we achieved significant milestones, delivering 12,134 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports models, through our distribution partner LTIL. Our workforce expanded to 1,996 full-time employees, with approximately 52% dedicated to Research and Development across teams in China and Europe. Financially, we experienced substantial revenue growth, with total revenues increasing from US$9.6 million in 2022 to US$924.3 million in 2024. However, this growth was accompanied by net losses, which escalated from US$724.6 million in 2022 to US$1,107.3 million in 2024.
